title: Lotoxalis frutescens
intro: Lotoxalis frutescens, also known as Creeping Jenny or Lotoxalis, is a perennial, flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalis family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Lotoxalis frutescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lotoxalis frutescens are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Lotoxalis frutescens is a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Oxalidaceae. This plant is native to the Americas and is also known as the woodland sorrel or wild sorrel. Its classification places it in the genus Lotoxalis, which is a type of flowering plant that produces small, white or pink flowers. The plant is also known to be a member of the order Oxalidales.
